title: Roblox Q1 2026: Beats Expectations With $1.4B Revenue and Record Bookings
text: Roblox reported total revenue of $1.4 billion for Q1 2026, representing a 39% year-over-year increase. Q1 showed robust revenue growth, bookings growth, exceptional cash-flow improvements, meaningful payer and engagement growth, expanding content diversity, and substantial AI and technology progress. However, Q1 2026 results showed a loss per share of $0.35, with revenue of $1.44 billion (up 39% from Q1 2025) and a net loss of $246.0 million (loss widened 14% from Q1 2025). Daily active users hit 132 million, up 35% year-over-year, with strong growth across all regions and age groups. Engagement hours grew to 31 billion hours. Despite strong financial metrics, the company faces significant headwinds. Strategic safety changes including global age checks and communication banding, discovery adjustments, and resulting reductions in app-store ratings have produced top-of-funnel weakness, a slowdown in DAU growth, and forced management to materially lower full-year bookings and revenue guidance. Multiple analysts have lowered their price targets following the earnings report and weak forward guidance, reflecting concerns about the impact of child safety measures on near-term growth.
